title: ResponseEnvelope
description: Standard wrapper for all command stdout output in JSON mode. Shape is invariant — the same keys are always present regardless of success, failure, or result count.

ok.description: True if and only if the exit code is SUCCESS (0). Derived field — do not allow commands to set this independently.

data.description: Primary command output. Present on success; null on failure. Never absent. Shape is command-specific and declared in that command's output schema (REQ-C-015).

error.description: Null on success. Structured error object on any non-zero exit.

warnings.description: Non-fatal diagnostic messages. May be empty. Never null.

code.description: Stable, machine-readable error identifier. Must not change between versions. Used by agents to branch on error type.

message.description: Human-readable summary. May be localized. Agents should not parse this — use 'code' instead.

detail.description: Extended explanation, stack context, or raw upstream error. Optional.

retryable.description: True if re-running the identical invocation unchanged may succeed; implies no side effects occurred. Mirrors ExitCodeEntry.retryable for the emitted code. False for caller-correctable failures — see fix_required.

retry_after_ms.description: Milliseconds the agent should wait before retrying. Present only when retryable is true and a specific back-off is known.

retry_strategy.description: Back-off strategy the agent should apply on retry. Present only when retryable is true.

fix_required.description: Condition the caller must correct before reissuing. Present only when retryable is false and the failure is caller-correctable; absent on terminal failures.

fix_command.description: Exact command invocation that resolves the fix_required condition, executable verbatim with no placeholders. Must be read-only or idempotent, never destructive. Present only when retryable is false and a single-command remediation exists.

phase.description: Pipeline phase in which the error occurred. 'validation' guarantees zero side effects.

suggestion.description: Actionable next step phrased for an agent. Optional.

redirect.description: Present only when exit code is REDIRECTED (13). Contains the replacement command.

command.description: Exact replacement invocation the agent MUST use verbatim on retry.

permanent.description: True if the mapping is permanent — agent should memorize and never call the old form again. False if temporary — use the replacement only for this request.

reason.description: Why the redirect exists. Informs whether the agent should update its schema knowledge.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock time in milliseconds from command entry to final byte of output.

request_id.description: Opaque identifier for correlating logs, traces, and audit entries.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the response envelope schema. Allows agents to detect breaking changes.

not_modified.description: True when an etag matched and data is intentionally null (cache hit).

truncated.description: True when the output was capped by the framework's size limit. Agents should paginate or narrow the query.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination token. Pass as --cursor on the next call to retrieve the next page.
